pending.body: finding_summary: reconcile_success emits incorrect summary counts because it diffs persisted state instead of using the actual reconciliation results. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:203-207 and 386-396, beads_created and planned_elsewhere_mappings are derived from before/after journal snapshots via helpers at 418-476. That miscounts common recovery paths: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3343-3351 appends ProposedBeadCreated even when replay is only recovering an already-created bead, and src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1018-1045 rewrites planned-elsewhere mappings in place during verification, so the summary can over-report created beads and report 0 planned-elsewhere mappings even when verification just succeeded. affected_files: ["src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]. remediation_hint: return explicit counters from reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success and verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success and log those, rather than inferring counts from durable-state deltas.

finding_summary: the new low-level br spans log full command lines, including free-form payload arguments, into the operation field. src/adapters/br_process.rs:321-323 turns every argv element into display_string(), and src/adapters/br_process.rs:655-748 records that string on exec_read/exec_mutation/exec_json spans. create/comment commands can carry long descriptions and review text (see src/adapters/br_process.rs:404-418 and create_bead's use of description arguments), so successful traces now emit unbounded user-authored content into logs instead of a bounded operation name. affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_process.rs"]. remediation_hint: keep operation to a bounded command/subcommand identifier and, if needed, log selected arguments in separate redacted fields rather than serializing the whole argv.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 3 -> Plan and Implement
